heading: Arto contra-cyclic follow-up queue isolates unvalidated local twin-circle and device-mapping notes beneath the stable lane
text:
type: observation
tags: [qa, arto-heino, contra-cyclic, followup-queue, sixto-ramos]
body: Built `arto_contra_cyclic_followup_queue.json` and `build_arto_contra_cyclic_followup_queue.py` to stack local prior work under the stable Arto contra-cyclic lane without promoting it prematurely. The queue has 3 items: (1) a high-priority Archimedean twin-circle formula audit from the 2024 radius note, flagged as internally unstable because it mixes `r = d*d - d*sqrt(F)` with later `r^2 = D^2` / scaled-tuple interpretations and inconsistent `(1,2,3,5)` numerics; (2) a high-priority Sixto Ramos device-mapping note from the 2025 QA mapping file, kept only as a hypothesis generator because it introduces trig/ellipse-on-torus/mod-24 scheduler claims not yet validated against a recovered diagram; and (3) medium-priority Ucros/Constantinesco follow-ups. Honest verdict: the next correct build is a source-graded Sixto Ramos geometry packet, not more freeform QA mapping.
